title: Building a Robust Portfolio Workflow
author: Nikola Jankovic
summary: How I moved my portfolio from static sections to modular data-driven pages with cleaner maintenance.
content: I wanted my portfolio to be easier to evolve, so I moved repeated content into data structures and rendered it with JavaScript. That made it simpler to maintain consistent layouts while still letting each section have its own style and behavior.
The biggest win was reducing friction for updates. Adding a project now means editing structured data, not rewriting markup in multiple places.

title: Designing Better Mobile Navigation
author: Nikola Jankovic
summary: A breakdown of refining mobile navigation for compact screens while preserving usability.
content: Compact screens demand stricter layout intent. I focused on predictable alignment and clear touch targets.
I used explicit positioning for logo, menu trigger, and dark mode controls to avoid accidental wrapping.
Small spacing decisions have a big impact on perceived quality and usability on phones.

title: Timeline-Based Project Storytelling
author: Nikola Jankovic
summary: Using timeline categories to make project status clearer: present, past, future, and dead.
content: Timeline filtering helps visitors understand what is active versus archived or dropped.
By pairing categories with visual cues, users can parse status quickly without reading every card.
The interaction became cleaner once each mode had one primary job: explore, filter, and inspect.
